human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for higher-education campus event lifecycle execution, participant and learner-record handling, study consent and IRB boundaries, accessibility and safety escalation, completion evidence, functionality coverage, research contract coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and education-and-research research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and education-and-research are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to campus event execution, including event registration, venue reservation, schedule, participant, learner-record, accessibility, safety, approval, delivery, exception, and completion evidence without academic, research ethics, registrar, safety, accessibility, legal, financial, or policy-override authority drift.

Authority sources cover FERPA learner-record handling, Common Rule research consent and IRB boundaries, Clery-informed campus safety routing, ADA Title II event accessibility constraints, HLC accreditation and assessment evidence caveats, and public university event workflow references.

Scenario set covers normal event execution, incomplete inputs, completion without evidence, study consent gaps, grading and instructional authority, FERPA-sensitive records, accessibility evidence, campus safety incidents, registrar and accreditation routing, realistic terminology, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant event policy, jurisdictional assembly rules, facility requirements, event-risk thresholds, IRB determinations, and accommodation procedures are treated as runtime retrieval dependencies with escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
